conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
That helps, honestly! So, what have you been up to this week?
Just the usual, work and stuff. I'm a dental hygienist at a clinic in Seattle.
Ah, that sounds interesting. What's your favorite part about your job?
Honestly, it's just the sense of accomplishment when patients leave with clean teeth. You know, they're happy and I'm happy too!
That must be very fulfilling! Do you have any fun plans or social events coming up this weekend?
Nah, not really. My friends are all busy with work and stuff, so it's been pretty low-key.
Okay, that makes sense. How's your sleep been lately? Are you getting a good night's rest?
Yeah, I mean, I sleep fine, to be honest. It's just that when I do wake up, I always feel exhausted.
That can be really tough! Do you notice any changes in your energy levels throughout the day?
Oh, definitely. Sometimes I'll get a burst of energy and then suddenly crash. And it's hard to focus on anything for too long.
Okay, got it. How about your appetite? Are you eating like usual or noticing any changes there?
Honestly, my stomach's been doing some weird things lately. I mean, sometimes I'll eat a big meal and then not be hungry again for hours.
That can be unsettling! Have you noticed anything else going on that might be affecting your appetite?
Nah, I don't think so. Maybe just stress from work and stuff. We've been short-staffed lately, and it's been tough to keep up.
I understand. It sounds like things have been pretty challenging at the clinic. How about your hobbies or interests outside of work? Are you doing anything that brings you joy?
Um, not really. I used to enjoy hiking on my days off, but lately, even that feels like too much effort.
Okay! Maybe we can explore some ways to get you enjoying those activities again. What's been going on at work that's making it hard for you to muster the energy?
Honestly, just trying to keep up with the patient load and meet all the demands from management... It feels like I'm constantly behind.
That can be really frustrating! Have you noticed any changes in your mood or emotional state lately? Feeling more down or anxious at all?
Yeah, definitely. Sometimes I'll just feel this crushing sense of guilt over things that aren't even my fault.
Okay, let's work on breaking down those feelings a bit. What specific situations have been making you feel guilty recently?
Like when patients don't come in for their appointments or leave negative reviews... It makes me feel like I'm not doing enough to help them.
I see. And how has that been affecting your daily life? Are there any other areas where you're struggling to concentrate or make decisions?
Yeah, definitely. Sometimes it takes me a really long time to get things done because my mind keeps wandering... It's hard for me to focus on one thing.
That must be really tough! How about physical sensations or body feelings? Have you noticed anything unusual going on in your body lately?
Honestly, I've been getting these weird muscle aches and pains. I think it might be related to stress or something.
Okay, that's definitely worth exploring further. What kind of recent stressful life events have you experienced? Anything significant happen recently?
Well, my mom was in the hospital for a while, and that really took a toll on me... But she's okay now, thankfully!
That must have been really tough for you! Who do you usually turn to when you need support or coping strategies?
Um, mostly just myself, I guess. My friends are all busy, but my mom's always been super supportive.
Okay! It sounds like your mom is a great source of comfort for you. What kind of goals would you like to set for yourself over the next few weeks? How do you want things to be different?
Honestly, I just want to feel more in control again... Like my life isn't being run by stress and anxiety.
That's a great starting point! Let's work on some strategies together to help you regain that sense of control.
